content.full_text:
California Fair Political Practices Commission
April 24, 1990

David Iverson
460 Poplar Street
Half Moon Bay, CA 94019

Re: Your Request for Further Advice
Our File No. A-90-067b

Dear Mr. Iverson:

In a prior letter (Yang Advice Letter, A-90-067) we advised you that the Political Reform Act (the "Act")¹ precluded your participation in decisions on the adoption of a proposed redevelopment plan in the City of Half Moon Bay. This advice was based on the fact that you owned your place of residence within the proposed redevelopment plan, that any city council decision on the plan would have a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on you or your real property interest, and that this effect was distinguishable from the public generally.

In your letter of April 10, 1990, in which you have indicated that you regard this advice as preliminary guidance only, you have requested further consideration of your question from the Commission. To that end, you have included in your letter what you believe are additional facts.²

The purpose of this letter is to inform you that, if you so request, we will submit your letter to the Commission for consideration at its meeting on June 6, 1990. Until you inform us that such is your intention, and until the Commission directs otherwise, our position is that the advice provided in A-90-067 remains the advice of the Commission, and you may not participate in the decisions concerning the city's redevelopment plan because of your ownership of your place of residence within the proposed redevelopment plan.

---

¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

² For example, you have raised questions concerning the state's Community Redevelopment Law. The Commission does not provide advice with respect to statutes other than the Act. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(D).)

In your telephone conversation with this agency on April 9, 1990, you were informed of the different types of advice provided by the Commission. The prior letter (A-90-067) constituted formal written advice pursuant to Section 83114(b) and Regulation 18329(b). Your letter of January 23, 1990 requested advice pursuant to Section 83114(b).

I have enclosed copies of Regulations 18320, 18321, 18322, 18324, 18326, 18327, and 18329 for your information. These regulations outline the opinion and advice procedures adopted by the Commission.

I trust this letter has provided you with the guidance you requested. If you have any further questions regarding this matter please contact me at (916) 322-5091.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

By: Jonathan S. Rothman
Counsel, Legal Division

KED:JSR:plh
cc: Iris Yang
Enclosures
